summary: Frontend-only smoke test of FlowSync landing page. Page loads with no console errors. Navbar, Hero (with animated dashboard), 6 Features, 4 Stats, 4 Benefits, 6 Testimonials, 3 Pricing plans with working monthly/yearly toggle (₹999/₹2,999 monthly, ₹799/₹2,399 yearly, Enterprise Custom), all 5 FAQ accordion triggers, and mobile menu (5 links + login + trial) all verified. Pricing nav link smoothly scrolls to #pricing. No horizontal scroll on mobile (390px). Two issues found: invalid-email toast is suppressed by HTML5 native validation, and 'Most Popular' badge text is actually 'Most popular' (lowercase p) - spec mismatch.
passed: landing-page renders with no console errors
Navbar with 5 desktop links + Login + Start Free Trial
Hero: 'Automate Workflows' headline, trial+demo buttons, hero-dashboard mockup
Benefits: 4 cards (Save Time, Reduce Costs, Improve Collaboration, Scale Faster)
Pricing: starter, pro, enterprise cards present
Billing toggle: yearly shows ₹799 & ₹2,399; monthly shows ₹999 & ₹2,999; Enterprise Custom
Newsletter: valid email clears input after submit
Mobile (390x844): mobile-menu-toggle opens menu with 5 links + mobile-login-btn + mobile-trial-btn
Pricing nav link smooth-scrolls to #pricing section

failed.issue: Submitting an invalid email like 'notanemail' triggers the browser's native HTML5 email validation tooltip ('Please include an @...'), which prevents the form's onSubmit handler from running. As a result, toast.error('Please enter a valid email address.') in Footer.jsx is never shown. Spec requires that invalid email shows an error toast.
failed.evidence: Screenshot shows native browser tooltip popup; sonner toaster section is empty (<section aria-label='Notifications...'></section>). Footer.jsx line 30 contains toast.error but is unreachable due to <input type='email' required>.

failed.issue: Spec requires 'Most Popular' badge but Pricing.jsx renders 'Most popular' (lowercase p). Badge IS visible on Pro plan but text casing differs from spec.
failed.evidence: grep on Pricing.jsx line 144: '<Sparkles ... /> Most popular'

action_items: Footer.jsx: change <input type='email'> to type='text' (or add noValidate on the form) so the custom regex check + toast.error fires for invalid emails like 'notanemail'.
Pricing.jsx line 144: change 'Most popular' to 'Most Popular' to match the spec exactly (optional/cosmetic).
